title: intensely pungent canadian duck wrap
description: a lazy-sunday canadian dish featuring tender duck, duck fat, and ssam-wrap. paleo-friendly and packed with flavor.
instructions: score duck breast in a crisscross pattern.
season with salt and black pepper.
place duck on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
roast duck in the oven for 20 minutes, or until cooked through.
while duck is cooking, heat duck fat in a skillet over medium heat.
add green onions and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
add toasted sesame seeds and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add soy sauce and honey, and stir to combine.
remove duck from the oven and let rest for 5 minutes.
assemble wraps by placing a few slices of duck onto a ssam-wrap, followed by a spoonful of the green onion and sesame seed mixture.
drizzle with soy sauce and honey mixture, and top with chopped cilantro.
